frameworks.name: inattentional blindness
frameworks.one_line: salient unexpected events go unseen when attention is engaged elsewhere

frameworks.name: selective attention
frameworks.one_line: task-focused attention gates which events reach awareness

core_question: How do selective attention and inattentional blindness disrupt eyewitness awareness and memory for crimes and accidents?
key_assumptions: attention is a limited resource allocated to a primary task
events that escape attention fail to enter durable memory
manipulations: observers engaged in a competing task vs. attending to the criminal scene
key_findings: task-focused observers often miss salient unexpected events (crime blindness)
memory for unattended events is correspondingly impaired
interpretations: legal procedures assuming comprehensive observation are unreliable when observers were not attending to the scene
